name: Yan Jian Rou (Chili Pork)
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 t Sichuan peppercorns, roasted and crushed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork into 3-inch thin slices and combine with the soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice wine, salt, and sesame oil.
2. Cut the chiles in half lengthwise and remove the seeds.
3. Heat a wok or large saute pan until hot. Add 1½ tablespoons of peanut oil and the pork, and stir-fry for 1 minute. Remove the pork with a slotted spoon.
4. Reheat the wok and add the remaining oil. When very hot, add the fresh chiles, garlic, chili powder or cayenne pepper, and Sichuan peppercorns, and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
5. Add water, 1 tablespoon rice wine, soy sauce, and sugar to the pan, and cook for another 30 seconds.
6. Return the pork to the wok and heat through. Give the mixture several good stirs.
7. Turn off the heat, transfer onto a platter, and serve. Serves 4 as part of a Chinese meal or 2 as a single dish.
